factors.summary: The industry is highly competitive, with significant competition from local and regional chains and private enterprises.

evidence.text: The building products supply industry is highly fragmented and competitive. We face, and will continue to face, significant competition from local and regional building materials chains, as well as from privately-owned single site enterprises.
evidence.subsection: Our industry is highly fragmented and competitive, and increased competitive pressure may adversely affect our results.

factors.summary: The company faces potential costs and operational restrictions due to regulatory changes.

evidence.text: More burdensome regulatory requirements in these or other areas may increase our general and administrative costs and adversely affect our financial condition, operating results and cash flows.
evidence.subsection: Federal, state, local and other regulations could impose substantial costs and/or restrictions on our ope rations that would reduce our net income.

factors.summary: The company is subject to environmental liabilities and regulations which could lead to increased costs.

evidence.text: We are subject to various federal, state and local environmental laws, ordinances and regulations. Although we believe that our facilities are in material compliance with such laws, as owners and lessees of real property, we can be held liable for the investigation or remediation of contamination on such properties.
evidence.subsection: We are subject to potential exposure to environmental liabilities and are subject to environmental regulation.

factors.summary: The integration of ProBuild presents significant challenges and risks, including potential cost overruns and operational disruptions.

evidence.text: Combining the operations of Builders First Source and ProBuild may be more difficult, costly or time consuming than expected and the anticipated benefits and cost savings of the ProBuild acquisition may not be realized.
evidence.subsection: We may be adversely affected by uncertainty in the economy and financial markets, including as a result of terrorism or unrest in the Middle East, Eastern Europe or elsewhere.

factors.summary: Approximately 2% of ProBuild's workforce is unionized, introducing potential labor relations risks.

evidence.text: Approximately 2% of the workforce at ProBuild are members of nine different unions. None of the workforce at our legacy Company was unionized.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, driven by increased demand and successful integration of recent acquisitions.

evidence.text: Our net sales for the third quarter of 2015 were up 193.4% over the same period last year, 188.9% of which was due to recent acquisitions, primarily the acquisition of ProBuild.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products, benefiting the company.

evidence.text: Excluding the impact of the ProBuild acquisition, our gross margin percentage increased 2.1%, primarily due to improved customer pricing relative to our costs and a higher mix of value-added sales.

factors.summary: Volume trends are growing, driven by increased demand and successful integration of ProBuild.

evidence.text: Our net sales for the third quarter of 2015 were up 193.4% over the same period last year, 188.9% of which was due to recent acquisitions, primarily the acquisition of ProBuild. Excluding the impact of recent acquisitions, we estimate net sales increased 11.8% due to increased volume.

factors.summary: The homebuilding market is the fastest-growing end market, with multi-family and light commercial markets also showing growth potential.

evidence.text: Our primary focus has been, and continues to be, on single-family residential new construction. However, we have recently acquired companies with multi-family manufacturing capabilities to further diversify our customer base. We will continue to identify opportunities for profitable growth in the multi-family and light commercial markets.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with attention to workforce productivity and cost efficiency.

evidence.text: We pay close attention to managing our working capital and operating expenses. We have a 'best practices' operating philosophy, which encourages increasing efficiency, lowering costs, improving working capital, and maximizing profitability and cash flow. We constantly analyze our workforce productivity to achieve the optimum, cost-efficient labor mix for our facilities.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are in place and contributing to efficiency, though some expenses have increased due to integration and amortization.

evidence.text: We made significant changes to our business during the downturn that have improved our operating efficiency and allowed us to better leverage our operating costs against changes in net sales. We intend to implement similar changes in ProBuild’s business activities to the extent feasible. However, our selling, general and administrative expenses, as a percentage of net sales, were 22.5% in the third quarter of 2015, a 3.7% increase from 18.8% in the third quarter of 2014. Excluding ProBuild, selling, general, and administrative expenses were 22.3% of net sales. The increase was primarily due to acquisition costs related to the ProBuild acquisition, an increase in facility closure costs associated with location consolidations, and an increase in intangible asset amortization.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving through best practices initiatives and workforce productivity.

evidence.text: We have a 'best practices' operating philosophy, which encourages increasing efficiency, lowering costs, improving working capital, and maximizing profitability and cash flow. We constantly analyze our workforce productivity to achieve the optimum, cost-efficient labor mix for our facilities.

factors.summary: Supply chain is functioning normally, with no significant disruptions mentioned.

evidence.text: The composition and level of working capital typically change during periods of increasing sales as we carry more inventory and receivables. Working capital levels typically increase in the second and third quarters of the year due to higher sales during the peak residential construction season. These increases have in the past resulted in negative operating cash flows during this peak season, which historically have been financed through available cash and borrowing availability under credit facilities. Collection of receivables and reduction in inventory levels following the peak building and construction season have in the past positively impacted cash flow.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing due to higher demand and successful integration of ProBuild.

evidence.text: The acquisition of ProBuild has substantially increased the scale of our company. Successfully integrating ProBuild will be critical to achieving our future objectives. Combining our two companies may be more difficult, costly, or time-consuming than expected, which could result in the acquisition not achieving its intended results, including the expected operational synergies and cost savings.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing, with attention to managing working capital.

evidence.text: Working capital levels typically increase in the second and third quarters of the year due to higher sales during the peak residential construction season. These increases have in the past resulted in negative operating cash flows during this peak season, which historically have been financed through available cash and borrowing availability under credit facilities. Collection of receivables and reduction in inventory levels following the peak building and construction season have in the past positively impacted cash flow.

factors.summary: No significant quality or safety issues reported, focusing on operational efficiency and customer satisfaction.

evidence.text: We pay close attention to managing our working capital and operating expenses. We have a 'best practices' operating philosophy, which encourages increasing efficiency, lowering costs, improving working capital, and maximizing profitability and cash flow. We constantly analyze our workforce productivity to achieve the optimum, cost-efficient labor mix for our facilities.

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance, expressing confidence in the long-term outlook despite near-term challenges.

evidence.text: We still believe that the long-term outlook for the housing industry is positive due to growth in the underlying demographics. We feel we are well-positioned to take advantage of the construction activity in our markets and to continue to increase our market share, which may include strategic acquisitions.

factors.summary: Management is cautiously optimistic, focusing on integration, cost control, and strategic growth.

evidence.text: We will continue to focus on working capital by closely monitoring the credit exposure of our customers and by working with our vendors to improve our payment terms and pricing on our products. We will also continue to work diligently to achieve the appropriate balance of short-term expense control while maintaining the expertise and capacity to grow the business as market conditions improve. We want to create long-term shareholder value and avoid taking steps that will limit our ability to compete.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include economic conditions, commodity price fluctuations, and successful integration of ProBuild.

evidence.text: The building products supply industry is highly dependent upon new home construction and subject to cyclical market changes. Our operations are subject to fluctuations arising from changes in supply and demand, national and local economic conditions, labor costs and availability, competition, government regulation, trade policies and other factors that affect the homebuilding industry such as demographic trends, interest rates, single-family housing starts, employment levels, consumer confidence, and the availability of credit to homebuilders, contractors, and homeowners.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy focuses on market expansion, integration of acquisitions, and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: We will continue to identify opportunities for profitable growth in the multi-family and light commercial markets. The successful integration of the ProBuild business is critical to achieving future objectives, including operational synergies and cost savings.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include economic conditions, interest rates, and housing market trends.

evidence.text: The building products supply industry is highly dependent upon new home construction and subject to cyclical market changes. Our operations are subject to fluctuations arising from changes in supply and demand, national and local economic conditions, labor costs and availability, competition, government regulation, trade policies and other factors that affect the homebuilding industry such as demographic trends, interest rates, single-family housing starts, employment levels, consumer confidence, and the availability of credit to homebuilders, contractors, and homeowners.

factors.summary: Successful integration of ProBuild and execution of cost-saving measures are key catalysts for future performance.

evidence.text: The successful integration of ProBuild will be critical to achieving our future objectives. Combining our two companies may be more difficult, costly, or time-consuming than expected, which could result in the acquisition not achieving its intended results, including the expected operational synergies and cost savings.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are focused on creating long-term value through growth and efficiency, with no specific mention of buybacks or dividends.

evidence.text: We want to create long-term shareholder value and avoid taking steps that will limit our ability to compete.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is under pressure due to seasonal factors and integration costs, but expected to improve with cost controls.

evidence.text: These increases have in the past resulted in negative operating cash flows during this peak season, which historically have been financed through available cash and borrowing availability under credit facilities. Collection of receivables and reduction in inventory levels following the peak building and construction season have in the past positively impacted cash flow.

factors.summary: Market share is stable with a focus on increasing it through strategic acquisitions and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: We feel we are well-positioned to take advantage of the construction activity in our markets and to continue to increase our market share, which may include strategic acquisitions.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are intense, with consolidation and significant competition from local and regional suppliers.

evidence.text: Our industry remains highly fragmented and competitive and we will continue to face significant competition from local and regional suppliers. However, we still believe there are several meaningful trends that indicate U.S. housing demand will likely recover further in the long term and that the downturn in the housing industry was likely a trough in the cyclical nature of the residential construction industry.

factors.summary: Differentiation is achieved through a focus on value-added products and strong customer relationships, particularly with large homebuilders.

evidence.text: Our focus is on maintaining relationships and market share with these customers while balancing the competitive pressures we are facing in servicing large homebuilders with certain profitability expectations. We expect that our ability to maintain strong relationships with the largest builders will be vital to our ability to expand into new markets as well as grow our market share.

factors.summary: No specific information on equipment replacement cycles is provided.

evidence.text: The composition and level of working capital typically change during periods of increasing sales as we carry more inventory and receivables. Working capital levels typically increase in the second and third quarters of the year due to higher sales during the peak residential construction season.

factors.summary: Dealer/distributor inventory levels are managed to support peak seasons, with no specific mention of excess or depletion.

evidence.text: Working capital levels typically increase in the second and third quarters of the year due to higher sales during the peak residential construction season. These increases have in the past resulted in negative operating cash flows during this peak season, which historically have been financed through available cash and borrowing availability under credit facilities.

factors.summary: No specific information on aftermarket/service revenue growth is provided.

evidence.text: The composition and level of working capital typically change during periods of increasing sales as we carry more inventory and receivables. Working capital levels typically increase in the second and third quarters of the year due to higher sales during the peak residential construction season.

factors.summary: No significant foreign exchange exposure mentioned in the text.

evidence.text: The composition and level of working capital typically change during periods of increasing sales as we carry more inventory and receivables. Working capital levels typically increase in the second and third quarters of the year due to higher sales during the peak residential construction season.

factors.summary: The company faces a significant customer concentration risk, particularly with large homebuilders, as indicated by the notable portion of sales generated by the top ten customers.

evidence.text: Our ten largest customers generated approximately 25.1% and 22.5% of our sales for the years ended December 31, 2014 and 2013, respectively.
evidence.subsection: The loss of any of our significant customers or a reduction in the quantity of products they purchase could affect our financial health.

evidence.text: Our business is driven primarily by both the residential new construction market and the residential repair and remodel market, which are in turn dependent upon a number of factors, including demographic trends, interest rates, consumer confidence, employment rates, foreclosure rates, and the health of the economy and mortgage markets.

factors.summary: The company is facing competitive pricing pressure from customers, particularly in the housing market downturn, which could negatively impact its pricing power. However, it has managed to hold prices effectively, contributing to improved gross margins, indicating some success in maintaining pricing power despite the pressures.

evidence.text: Production homebuilders and commercial builders historically have exerted and will continue to exert significant pressure on their outside suppliers to keep prices low because of their market share and their ability to leverage such market share in the highly fragmented building products supply industry.
evidence.subsection: We are subject to competitive pricing pressure from our customers.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage increased by 3.0% during the third quarter of 2015 compared to the third quarter of 2014. Excluding the impact of the ProBuild acquisition, our gross margin percentage increased 2.1%, primarily due to improved customer pricing relative to our costs and a higher mix of value-added sales.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly lumber prices, are experiencing volatility and a downward trend, as evidenced by a 10.6% decline over nine months and a 15.9% decrease in commodity prices over three months. However, the company has implemented strategies such as pricing adjustments and productivity improvements to manage these fluctuations, which may help mitigate the impact on operating income.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2015, average prices for lumber and lumber sheet goods were 10.6% lower than the prior year.
evidence.subsection: The building supply industry is cyclical and seasonal.

evidence.text: The $3.4 million decline in net sales of lumber and lumber sheet goods, excluding ProBuild, was largely due to a 15.9% decrease in market prices for such commodities in the third quarter of 2015 compared to the third quarter of 2014.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2014

evidence.text: Prices of wood products, which are subject to cyclical market fluctuations, may adversely impact operating income when prices rapidly rise or fall within a relatively short period of time.

factors.summary: Demand trends in the building products supply industry are improving, supported by increases in housing starts and construction activity, though the industry remains cyclical and subject to economic conditions.

evidence.text: The building products supply industry is subject to cyclical market pressures. Prices of building products are subject to fluctuations arising from changes in supply and demand, national and international economic conditions, labor costs, competition, market speculation, government regulation, and trade policies, as well as from periodic delays in the delivery of lumber and other products.
evidence.subsection: The building supply industry is cyclical and seasonal.

evidence.text: According to the U.S. Census Bureau, actual U.S. single-family housing starts increased 14.5% and single-family units under construction increased 13.3% in the third quarter of 2015 compared to the third quarter of 2014.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2014

evidence.text: The homebuilding industry has shown improvement since 2011. For the third quarter of 2015, actual U.S. single-family housing starts were 203,400, a 14.5% increase compared to the third quarter of 2014.

factors.summary: Interest rates are having a mixed impact on the company's operations. While low interest rates are supporting housing demand, they are also increasing the company's borrowing costs due to variable-rate debt under the 2015 facility.

evidence.text: Borrowings under the 2015 facility bear interest at a variable rate. Therefore, actual interest may differ from the amounts presented above due to any future borrowing activity under the 2015 facility.
evidence.subsection: DISCLOSURES OF CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATIONS AND COMMERCIAL COMMITMENTS

evidence.text: These trends include relatively low interest rates, the aging of housing stock, and normal population growth due to immigration and birthrate exceeding death rate. Industry forecasters, including the National Association of Homebuilders (''NAHB''), expect to see continued improvement in housing demand over the next few years.

factors.summary: Construction infrastructure spending is growing, driven by an improving housing market, though it remains below historical averages. The industry's performance is influenced by the broader U.S. economy, including factors such as interest rates, consumer confidence, employment rates, and the health of mortgage markets.

evidence.text: Construction activity is influenced by the residential homebuilding industry and the broader U.S. economy.
evidence.subsection: The industry in which we operate is dependent upon the residential homebuilding industry, as well as the U.S. economy, the credit markets and other important factors.

evidence.text: The NAHB is forecasting 719,000 U.S. single-family housing starts for 2015, which is up approximately 11.1% from 2014, but still well below the historical average (from 1959 to 2014) of 1.0 million single-family housing starts per year.

The building products industry is highly dependent on new home and multifamily construction, which in turn are dependent upon a number of factors, including interest rates, consumer confidence, employment rates, foreclosure rates, housing inventory levels and occupancy, housing demand and the health of the U.S. economy and mortgage markets.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage has significantly increased, primarily due to the ProBuild acquisition, resulting in higher interest expenses and reduced financial flexibility. While there is a focus on repaying debt, the increased leverage poses risks, such as potential impairment charges and limited ability to respond to economic downturns or changing conditions.

evidence.text: As of September 30, 2015, our debt totaled $2,074.6 million, including $289.6 million of lease finance obligations and capitalized lease obligations. We have an $800 million senior secured revolving credit facility ('2015 facility').
evidence.subsection: If the housing market declines, we may be required to take impairment charges relating to our operations or temporarily idle or permanently close under-performing locations.

evidence.text: Our capital resources at September 30, 2015 consist of cash on hand and borrowing availability under our revolving credit facility. We have substantially increased indebtedness following completion of the ProBuild acquisition in comparison to our indebtedness on a recent historical basis, which will increase our interest expense and could have the effect of, among other things, reducing our flexibility to respond to changing business and economic conditions.

Our consolidated indebtedness as of September 30, 2015 was approximately $2,074.6 million. We substantially increased our indebtedness in connection with the ProBuild acquisition in comparison to our indebtedness on a recent historical basis, which has increased our interest expense and could have the effect of, among other things, reducing our flexibility to respond to changing business and economic conditions.
evidence.subsection: The integration of ProBuild involves substantial costs.

evidence.text: As a result of the ProBuild acquisition, we have substantially increased indebtedness. Reduction of our outstanding debt will be a key imperative as we work to achieve the intended results of the acquisition. During the third quarter of 2015, we repaid $160.0 million of the original $295.0 million borrowed under the 2015 facility at the closing of the acquisition.

factors.summary: The economic conditions present a mixed outlook for the company, with favorable trends such as increased housing starts and construction activity, yet ongoing challenges including slow recovery, limited credit availability, and pressure on gross margins. While there is optimism about long-term recovery driven by factors like low interest rates and population growth, the industry continues to face competition and fragmentation.

evidence.text: The homebuilding industry is currently experiencing a shortage of qualified, trained labor in many areas, including those served by us. In addition, the building industry is subject to various local, state, and federal statutes, ordinances, rules and regulations concerning zoning, building design and safety, construction, energy conservation and similar matters.
evidence.subsection: The industry in which we operate is dependent upon the residential homebuilding industry, as well as the U.S. economy, the credit markets and other important factors.

evidence.text: According to the U.S. Census Bureau, actual U.S. single-family housing starts increased 14.5% and single-family units under construction increased 13.3% in the third quarter of 2015 compared to the third quarter of 2014.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2014

evidence.text: The building industry started to strengthen in 2011. According to the U.S. Census Bureau, the annualized rate for U.S. single-family housing starts was 740,000 as of September 30, 2015. However, single-family housing starts remain well below the historical average (from 1959 through 2014) of 1.0 million per year. Due to the lower levels of housing starts and increased competition for homebuilder business, we have and will continue to experience pressure on our gross margins.
Industry forecasters, including the National Association of Homebuilders (''NAHB''), expect to see continued improvement in housing demand over the next few years.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is showing a mixed trend with a deceleration in growth despite stable underlying performance. While there is an 11.8% increase in net sales excluding the impact of acquisitions, this growth is partially offset by a 7.3% decrease due to commodity price deflation, leading to a net effect that indicates a slowdown in organic growth.

evidence.text: Excluding the impact of acquisitions, net sales increased $19.5 million, or 11.8%, due to increased volume, which was partially offset by a 7.3% decrease due to the impact of commodity price deflation on net sales.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2014

evidence.text: Excluding the impact of recent acquisitions, we estimate net sales increased 11.8% due to increased volume, which was partially offset by a 7.3% decrease due to the impact of commodity price deflation on net sales.

factors.summary: Gross margins are improving and expanding, driven by better customer pricing relative to costs and a higher mix of value-added sales. The improvement is consistent across different subsections of the filing.

evidence.text: Our gross margin percentage increased to 25.5% in the third quarter of 2015 from 22.5% in the third quarter of 2014, a 3.0% increase. Excluding ProBuild, our gross margin percentage increased 2.1%, primarily due to improved customer pricing relative to our costs and a higher mix of value-added sales in the third quarter of 2015 compared to the third quarter of 2014.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2014

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage increased by 3.0% during the third quarter of 2015 compared to the third quarter of 2014. Excluding the impact of the ProBuild acquisition, our gross margin percentage increased 2.1%, primarily due to improved customer pricing relative to our costs and a higher mix of value-added sales.

factors.summary: Mergers and acquisitions, particularly the ProBuild acquisition, are driving growth by increasing net sales and expanding product offerings across more states. However, this activity is also leading to increased debt and higher interest expenses.

evidence.text: Due to the ProBuild acquisition, we achieved increased net sales across all product categories. Our sales classification by product categories has shifted as we diversified our product offerings to support a broader customer mix across 40 states through the ProBuild acquisition.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2014

evidence.text: The ProBuild acquisition has substantially increased the scale of our company. We believe that the ProBuild acquisition will lead to greater diversification and scale, an improved geographic footprint, and significant potential cost savings.

factors.summary: The company faces potential risks from geopolitical instability in regions like the Middle East and Eastern Europe, which could impact its operations. However, there is no significant mention of geopolitical risks in other sections.

evidence.text: Instability in the economy and financial markets, including as a result of terrorism or unrest in the Middle East, Eastern Europe or elsewhere, may result in a decrease in housing starts, which would adversely affect our business.
evidence.subsection: We may be adversely affected by uncertainty in the economy and financial markets, including as a result of terrorism or unrest in the Middle East, Eastern Europe or elsewhere.

evidence.text: The composition and level of working capital typically change during periods of increasing sales as we carry more inventory and receivables. Working capital levels typically increase in the second and third quarters of the year due to higher sales during the peak residential construction season.

factors.summary: The company is increasing capital expenditures to support sales growth, expand capacity, and integrate acquisitions, reflecting confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: The increase in capital expenditures primarily relates to purchasing machinery, equipment and vehicles to support sales growth and to increase capacity at existing locations.

The ProBuild purchase price was funded with the net cash proceeds from (i) the sale of $700.0 million in aggregate principal amount of 10.75% senior unsecured notes due 2023 (''2023 notes''), (ii) the entry into a new $600.0 million term loan credit agreement (''2015 term loan''), (iii) a $295.0 million draw on an amended and restated $800.0 million senior secured revolving credit facility (''2015 facility''), and (iv) a public offering of 9.2 million new shares of our common stock at an offering price of $12.80 per share, subject, in each case, to applicable discounts, commissions, fees, and expenses.
